messages.content: Why do people take notes when they read?

People take notes when they read for several reasons, including:

1. To enhance comprehension: Taking notes help readers comprehend the text better by allowing them to actively engage with the material. 

2. To remember important information: Notes help readers remember key points and details from the text. 

3. To organize thoughts: Note-taking can help organize thoughts and ideas. 

4. To keep track of references: Notes help readers keep track of important references like quotes, sources, and authors. 

5. To aid in studying: Notes serve as a useful reference when studying for exams or writing papers. 

Overall, note-taking is a valuable tool that helps readers stay focused, retain information, and improve their understanding of the text.

on the basketball court. In one game in 2021, for example, the legendary Laker racked up 30 points.
After the game, a reporter asked what powered up his
performance. "Sleep,"
James replied. A good rest made him "able to come in with some energy and put it on the floor."
Before they can rise and shine, teens need 8 to 10 hours of sleep. Credit: Riska/Getty Images
James isn't the only one who recognizes the benefits of slumber.
Doctors and scientists say getting plenty of sleep can improve health Doctors and scientists say getting plenty of sleep can improve health and help people do better in sports, school, work, and just about anything else.
How Much Sleep Do People Need?
Not everyone needs the same amount of sleep. There are lots of reasons, but age has a lot to do with it. As people get older, how much sleep they need changes. The CDC is a U.S. public health agency. Its experts recommend the following daily amounts of sleep:
• 6 to 12 years old: 9 to 12 hours
• 13 to 18 years old: 8 to 10 hours
• over 18 years old: at least 7 hours
However, many people don't meet those numbers. That's especially true of teens. According to the CDC, almost 80 percent of high school students don't get enough sleep. On average, high school students get only 6.7 hours per night. That's partly because most schools start early. Other reasons include homework, jobs, and activities like What Good Is Sleep?
Ever heard the saying "If you snooze, you lose"? It's not true about health and well-being! Getting plenty of rest has lots of benefits
Sleep gives you energy. It helps the body heal itself and recover from everyday damage to muscles and bones. Doctors say it may help prevent some serious diseases as well as common illnesses like colds and flu. It's good for mental health, too. Studies show that sleep improves mood and reduces stress
Sleep also fires up the brain. People are better able to doncentrate and solve problems after a good night's rest. Both of those are essential to memory and learning. That's why experts say that if you've got nine precious hours to prepare for an all-important exam, you're better off bedding down early than staying up late studying.  sleep well, try these tips from experts.
• Get some sunshine and physical exercise during the day.
. Switch off screens at least 30 minutes before bedtime. The light they give off can signal your brain to stay awake.
Try to go to bed around the same time each night.
• Create an environment that's as dark and quiet as possible. Turn off lights and put electronics in silent mode.
Will getting plenty of sleep grant you the ability to slam dunk a basketball like LeBron? Probably not after all, there's goly one King
James. It could, however, give you a chance to be the healthiest happiest, most winning version of yourself.

As an AI language model, I do not have beliefs, emotions, or personal experiences. However, I can assist you by summarizing the passage you have provided.

The article talks about the importance of sleep and the benefits that come with it. The legendary basketball player LeBron James mentions that sleep powered up his performance in a game in 2021. Doctors and scientists agree that getting plenty of sleep can improve health and help people do better in sports, school, work, and other things. Different age groups need different amounts of sleep, and not everyone reaches the recommended amount. High school students are found to have less sleep due to school early hours, homework, jobs, and activities. Sleep provides energy, helps the body heal itself, prevents diseases, improves mood and reduces stress, and fires up the brain, which is essential for memory and learning. Lastly, some tips for sleeping well are given, including getting physical exercise during the day, turning off screens, and creating a dark and quiet environment.

The article talks mainly about _
A. why sleep is important and how to get the right
amount
B. why sleep helps concentration and how to study for
tests
C. how many nightly hours of sleep teens get on average
D. how much daily exercise people need to sleep well
A. why sleep is important and how to get the right amount.

Think about the article. How are teens and younger children different?
A. Teens need less sleep than younger children.
B. Teens find it easier to get up early than younger children.
C. Teens need more sleep than younger children
D. Teens find it easier to switch off screens than younger children.

C. Teens need more sleep than younger children. The article states that as people get older, how much sleep they need changes. The recommended daily amount of sleep for younger children and teens is different, with younger children needing more sleep than teens.

Read this passage from the article:
[Sleep is] good for mental health, too. Studies show that sleep improves mood and reduces stress.
In this passage, the word mental means having to do with
A. eyesight and hearing
B. muscles and bones
C. diet and exercise
D. thoughts and feelings

D. thoughts and feelings. The word 'mental' refers to the aspect related to the mind or related to the intellectual and emotional processes of a person, which include thoughts and feelings. The passage states that sleep is good for mental health, which means that it is beneficial for the well-being of a person's cognitive and emotional abilities.

Which sentence from the article best supports the idea that getting enough sleep can help make people happier?
A. Studies show that sleep improves mood and reduces stress.
B. As people get older, how much sleep they need
changes.
C. The light [screens] give off can signal your brain to stay awake.
D. On average, high school students get only 6.7 hours [of sleep] per night.
A. Studies show that sleep improves mood and reduces stress. This sentence clearly states that sleep is beneficial for a person's mood and can help people become happier.

Which of these is an opinion?
A. School and jobs are among the causes for the lack of sleep among teens.
B. The CDC recommends daily amounts of sleep for people based on their age.
C. Getting enough slep has a bigger effect on health than getting exercise.
D. LeBron says that sleep gives him the energy he needs to perform.

C. Getting enough sleep has a bigger effect on health than getting exercise. This statement is an opinion as different people may have different views or beliefs about the relative importance of sleep and exercise on health. The other options provided are factual statements.

Based on the article, the reader can tell that
A. the secret to doing well in school is to give up studying entirely and sleep instead
B. countries and communities are likely to benefit if their people get enough sleep
C. getting the recommended hours of sleep is only important for young people
D. eating healthy foodslis unnecessary for people with good sleep habits

B. Countries and communities are likely to benefit if their people get enough sleep. The article mentions how getting enough sleep can improve health and help people do better in various fields like sports, school, and work. It also highlights the benefits of sleep on mental health and reducing stress. These factors can positively impact communities and countries if more people are well-rested, leading to better productivity, health, and well-being. The other options provided are incorrect or inaccurate based on the information provided in the article.

This article would help a student write an essay on
8 / 8
A. the best types of exercise
B. famous athletes of the past
C. healthy lifestyle choices
D. the sleep habits of babies

C. Healthy lifestyle choices. The article focuses on the importance of sleep for maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mentions the benefits of sleep on various aspects like physical health, mental health, concentration, and memory. The article also provides tips for sleeping well, which can be useful for students writing about healthy lifestyle choices.

As you read this article, what is one question you had? It might be about a word or an idea.
Type your answer into the box.
As an AI language model, I cannot have questions or doubts. However, I am here to assist you with any queries or clarification you may need regarding the article you have provided.

Use your notes to revisit the article's content and your own thinking about it while you read.
• List the three key ideas from the article.
• Write one question you still have about an idea or a word in the article and where you might find an answer.
• Quote one passage you found interesting or important. Explain why you chose it.

Three key ideas from the article are:

1. Getting enough sleep is essential for overall well-being and health. It can improve physical health, mental health, mood, and reduce stress.
2. Different age groups need different amounts of sleep, and not everyone meets the recommended amount. Teens, in particular, tend to have less sleep due to school early hours, homework, jobs, and activities.
3. Tips for sleeping well include getting physical exercise during the day, turning off screens before bedtime, going to bed at the same time each night, and creating a calm and dark environment.

One question I still have is - What are the other benefits of sleep on physical health besides muscular and bone healing? An answer to this can be found by consulting other reliable sources on the topic.

One passage I found interesting is, "Studies show that sleep improves mood and reduces stress." I chose this passage because it highlights the importance of sleep on mental health, which is often overlooked. It emphasizes how getting enough sleep can lead to improved mood and reduce stress, which can significantly impact a person's overall well-being.

Based on information in the article, teenagers and younger children are similar in all of these ways except
A. both can enjoy improved mood with sufficient sleep
B. both need nine to twelve hours of sleep every day
C. both can focus better after adequate rest
D. both require sleep for the body to repair itself

B. Both need nine to twelve hours of sleep every day. This statement is incorrect as the article notes that different age groups need different amounts of sleep. Children aged 6 to 12 need 9 to 12 hours of sleep, teenagers from 13 to 18 need 8 to 10 hours of sleep, and adults over 18 years of age need at least 7 hours of sleep.

salubrious effects of slumber. Doctors and scientists say sufficient sleep can improve physical and mental health and enable people to do better in sports, school, work, and just about any other endeavor.
Which is the closest synonym for the word salubrious?
A. artificial
B. negative
C. temporary
D. beneficial
D. Beneficial. The word salubrious refers to something healthy, favorable, or beneficial to one's health or well-being. Among the options provided, the closest synonym for this word is "beneficial."

Which sentence from the article best supports the idea that developing certain habits can help people improve their quality of sleep?
A. While the amount of sleep individual people require varies, age is a major consideration.
B. Research shows that getting a good dose...reduces stress, while inadequate sleep has been linked to depression and anxiety.
C. Sleep...enables the body to heal itself and recover from everyday wear and tear.
D. Get some sunshine and plysical exercise during the

D. Get some sunshine and physical exercise during the day. This sentence provides a specific tip for developing good habits that can help improve the quality of sleep. It suggests getting physical exercise during the day can be useful in promoting good sleep, along with other tips provided in the article to aid better sleeping habits.

Which of these is a statement of opinion?
A. Getting enough sleep has a more significant impact on health than getting regular exercise.
B. The CDC provides daily sleep recommendations for people based on their age.
C. School and work schedules are among the causes for a lack of sleep among adolescents.
D. LeBron James follows a routine that includes getting eight to ten hours af sleep per night.

A. Getting enough sleep has a more significant impact on health than getting regular exercise. This statement is a matter of opinion and may vary among individuals based on their beliefs or personal experiences, whereas the other options provided are factual statements from the article.

Based on the article, the reader can infer that
A. getting sufficient daily sleep eliminates the need to eat a nutritious diet
B. society as a whole is likely to benefit from its members getting sufficient sleep
C. the secret to long-term academic success is to give up studying entirely and sled instead
D. getting the recommended hours of sleep is only important to young people

B. Society as a whole is likely to benefit from its members getting sufficient sleep. The article mentions the numerous health benefits of getting enough sleep, including physical, mental, and cognitive health. It also states that good sleep habits can impact a person's performance in sports, school, and work. Therefore, it can be inferred that if more people sleep well and maintain healthy sleep habits, then society as a whole would benefit from healthier, happier and more productive individuals. The other options provided either lack any factual support or are directly contradicted by the information in the article.

Which is the closest antonym for the word deficit, as it is used in the article?
7 / 8
A. necessity
B. surplus
C. precision
D. scarcity

B. Surplus. The word "deficit" refers to a shortage or insufficient amount of something. The closest antonym among the options given is "surplus," which means an amount that is more than needed or an excess of something.

This article would be most useful as a source for a student research project on
A. healthy lifestyle choices
B. sleep patterns in infants
C. famous athletes of the past
D. how to conduct sleep studies

A. Healthy lifestyle choices. The article primarily focuses on the importance of sleep in maintaining a healthy lifestyle, including the physical and mental health benefits of adequate sleep. The article provides tips for developing good sleeping habits and suggests that different age groups require different amounts of sleep. It also highlights the issues faced by teenagers and young people in getting sufficient amounts of sleep, offering suggestions on how to overcome those problems. Therefore, it would be most useful for a student researching healthy lifestyle choices rather than the other options listed.

What new technique for selling newspapers contributed to the outbreak of * 2 points the Spanish-American War?
• Social Darwinism
• yellow journalism
• muckraking
• jingoism
Yellow journalism contributed to the outbreak of the Spanish-American War.

What argument did American imperialists make in favor of the annexation * 2 points of Hawaii, Guam, and the Philippines?
these territories were necessary for protection of the Panama Canal of
these territories would provide good coaling stations for trade with East Asia
O the United States 'should prepare them for self-government and independence
the local populations of these territories had asked to be annexed
American imperialists argued that these territories would provide good coaling stations for trade with East Asia.

Philippines under Treaty of

What did African American writers, artists, and musicians hope to achieve * 2 points during the Harlem Renaissance?
to display their ability as conscientious workers in the workplace
to reveal that conditions for African Americans in the South were just as bad in the
• to show that the pursuit of material success can often lead to tragedy
• to demonstrate that African Americans could produce great works of literature and
What was the goal of the Washington Naval Conference, the Geneva Disarmament Conference and the Kellogg-Briand Pact?
• to strengthen U.S. naval power relative to the European power and Japan
to promote American investment abroad
to achieve lasting peace without the League of Nations
to prepare the Western Allies against German rearmament

African American writers, artists, and musicians hope to demonstrate that African Americans could produce great works of literature and art during the Harlem Renaissance.

The goal of the Washington Naval Conference, the Geneva Disarmament Conference, and the Kellogg-Briand Pact was to achieve lasting peace without the League of Nations.
